title: Floor-based person monitoring system
abstract: A person monitoring system comprises a resilient floor covering arranged in a monitored area, one or more pressure sensors installed in or under the resilient floor covering for generating an analog measurement signal and a detector for detecting person-related events in the monitored area. The detector includes an analog-to-digital converter for sampling the analog measurement signal and producing a digital measurement signal, a buffer memory for buffering the digital measurement signal and a processor for processing the digital measurement signal. The processor is configured to extract a feature vector from the buffered digital measurement signal and to map the extracted feature vector into one of at least two categories indicating the occurrence or the absence of a person-related event.
claims:
Claims WO 2017/216313 <| Q PCT/EP2017/064714 Claims 1 . A person monitoring system, comprising a resilient floor covering arranged in a monitored area; one or more pressure sensors installed in or under said resilient floor covering for generating an analog measurement signal; a detector for detecting person-related events in the monitored area, the detector including an analog-to-digital converter for sampling the analog measurement signal and producing a digital measurement signal, a buffer memory for buffering the digital measurement signal and a processor for processing the digital measurement signal; wherein the processor is configured to extract a feature vector from the buffered digital measurement signal and to map the extracted feature vector into one of at least two categories indicating the occurrence or the absence of a person-related event. 2. The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the buffer memory implements a FIFO buffer, wherein when the capacity of the FIFO buffer is reached, insertion of a new sample of the digital measurement signal into the FIFO buffer is accompanied with deletion of the oldest sample of the digital measurement signal from the FIFO buffer. 3. The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 2, and wherein the processor is configured to extract the feature vector from the entire content of the FIFO buffer and to map the extracted feature vector into one of at least two categories at the sampling rate. 4. The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 2, and wherein the processor is configured to extract the feature vector from the entire content of the FIFO buffer and to map the extracted feature vector into one of at least two categories each time n new samples have entered the FIFO buffer, wherein n = 1 , 2, 3, N and N is the size of the FIFO buffer. 5. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the processor is configured to use a classifier that splits the feature space in sub- spaces. 6. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the processor is configured to use a supervised classification algorithm. 

 WO 2017/216313 <| g PCT/EP2017/064714 7. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the processor is configured to use a K-nearest-neighbour classifier to map the extracted feature vector into one of the at least two categories. 8. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the processor is configured to use a random forest classifier to map the extracted feature vector into one of the at least two categories. 9. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the processor is configured to combine at least two of a K-nearest-neighbour classifier, threshold-based classifier and a random forest classifier to map the extracted feature vector into one of the at least two categories. 10. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the processor is configured to carry out a validation of the mapping of the extracted feature vector into one of the at least two categories, the validation taking into account previous feature vectors and/or previous classification decisions. 1 1 . The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 10, wherein the validation takes into account relationships between the at least two categories, the relationships comprising mutually excluding categories. 12. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the processor is configured to detect one or more of the following person-related events: fall of a person, a person walking, a person entering the monitored area, a person leaving the monitored area, a person leaving a first sub-area of the monitored area and entering a second sub-area of the monitored area. 13. The person monitoring system as claimed in any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the one or more pressure sensors are configured to convert variations in pressure exerted thereon into an electric signal that serves as the analog measurement signal. 14. The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 13, wherein the one or more pressure sensors comprise one or more sheet-type pressure sensors, each comprising a ferroelectret polymer film sandwiched between a first electrode layer and a second electrode layer. 15. The person monitoring system as claimed in claim 14, wherein the one or more pressure sensors comprise plural sheet-type pressure sensors arranged in substantially non-overlapping manner in different sub-areas of the monitored area.
